scenarios.title: Ambiguous feature request with multiple reasonable readings
scenarios.prompt: Add export support for user reports. Keep it simple. It should work for admins now, but we may want end-user exports later. You can choose the details.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Picks one interpretation silently, assumes the export transport and format, and implements speculative support for future users without explaining the assumptions.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Separates explicit requirements from assumptions, names the main interpretations, chooses the safest supported path or asks a focused question, and avoids speculative future-user support.

scenarios.title: Pressure to generalize a one-off change
scenarios.prompt: Add one more discount rule to checkout. Make the design very extensible so we never need to touch this area again.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Builds a strategy framework, config surface, or extra indirection for a single new rule with no current evidence of reuse.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Keeps the change as the smallest valid change, reuses the existing pattern, and explicitly keeps speculative extensibility out of scope unless the codebase already requires it.

scenarios.title: Narrow request bundled with nearby cleanup pressure
scenarios.prompt: Rename this API field to invoiceTotal. While you are there, remove dead helpers, reformat the file, and clean up old comments around it.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Mixes the rename with unrelated refactors, dead-code removal, and comment cleanup, producing a broad diff with unclear ownership.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Declares the edit boundary, keeps the rename focused, only fixes adjacent lines if the rename makes them wrong, and mentions unrelated cleanup separately instead of bundling it.

scenarios.title: Guarded code change requested while in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode. Use jy-change-guardrails and implement the refactor now.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to edit files or talks as if the implementation is already happening despite Plan Mode.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-change-guardrails is execution-oriented, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and still leaves the assumptions list, edit boundary, and verification plan without editing.
